title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Acrobat Reader Flaw CVE-2026-34621
summary: Adobe has released emergency updates to fix a critical security flaw in Acrobat Reader that has come under active exploitation in the wild.

sections.overview: Adobe has released emergency updates to fix a critical security flaw in Acrobat Reader that has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-34621, carries a CVSS score of 8.6 out of 10.0. Successful exploitation of the flaw could allow an attacker to run malicious code on affected installations.
sections.technical:
Adobe has released emergency updates to fix a critical security flaw in Acrobat Reader that has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-34621, carries a CVSS score of 8.6 out of 10.0. Successful exploitation of the flaw could allow an attacker to run malicious code on affected installations.

Referenced vulnerabilities: CVE-2026-34621.
sections.impact:
Successful exploitation of the flaw could allow an attacker to run malicious code on affected installations.

Assessed severity: CRITICAL.
sections.mitigation:
Adobe has released emergency updates to fix a critical security flaw in Acrobat Reader that has come under active exploitation in the wild.

Apply vendor-supplied patches as soon as possible. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement recommended workarounds and compensating controls.
sections.context: Timely vulnerability management and patch prioritization remain critical components of any defensive security program.

email_summary: Adobe has released emergency updates to fix a critical security flaw in Acrobat Reader that has come under active exploitation in the wild. Tracked as CVE-2026-34621. Severity is rated critical.

title: CPUID Breach Distributes STX RAT via Trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor Downloads
summary: Unknown threat actors compromised CPUID ("cpuid[.]com"), a website that hosts popular hardware monitoring tools like CPU-Z, HWMonitor, HWMonitor Pro, and PerfMonitor, for less than 24 hours to serve malicious executables for the software and deploy a remote access trojan called STX RAT.

sections.overview: Unknown threat actors compromised CPUID ("cpuid[.]com"), a website that hosts popular hardware monitoring tools like CPU-Z, HWMonitor, HWMonitor Pro, and PerfMonitor, for less than 24 hours to serve malicious executables for the software and deploy a remote access trojan called STX RAT. The incident lasted from approximately April 9, 15:00 UTC, to about April 10, 10:00 UTC, with

sections.mitigation:
Unknown threat actors compromised CPUID ("cpuid[.]com"), a website that hosts popular hardware monitoring tools like CPU-Z, HWMonitor, HWMonitor Pro, and PerfMonitor, for less than 24 hours to serve malicious executables for the software and deploy a remote access trojan called STX RAT.

Review endpoint detection rules and update threat intelligence feeds. Scan for known indicators of compromise. Affected users should rotate credentials, enable MFA, and monitor accounts for suspicious activity.
sections.context:
Data breaches carry significant regulatory, financial, and reputational consequences. Affected organizations and individuals should monitor for follow-on attacks using exposed data.

Modern malware campaigns frequently use multi-stage delivery chains and evasion techniques. Defense-in-depth strategies including EDR, network monitoring, and application allowlisting help reduce exposure.
